name: Route of the Mills Trail
name_original: Ruta de los Molinos – circular por el Castañar de Montánchez (PR-CC 27)
description: Hike the moderate 8.2-mile Route of the Mills (PR-CC 27) through Montánchez Chestnut Grove, exploring historic mills and scenic views.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Montánchez traverse the Sierra de Montánchez, a region characterized by granite hills rising from 300m to 994m. The landscape features low-lying pastures with olive and fig trees, transitioning to higher slopes covered with deciduous oak and chestnut woods. Interconnecting footpaths, some granite-paved, explore natural features such as the Montánchez Chestnut Grove and the Gorge of the Mills. Best hiking trails around Montánchez The most popular hiking route is Route of the Mills Trail, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route leads through the Montánchez Chestnut Grove, exploring historic mills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Montánchez – circular by the Ruta de las Aguas (PR-CC 30), a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path. This circular route incorporates water features and offers views of Montánchez Castle. Local hikers also love the Montánchez Castle – Montánchez Castle loop from Montánchez, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) difficult trail leading around the historic castle, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Hiking around Montánchez is defined by granite hills, oak and chestnut woods, and historic mills. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging climbs. The routes in Montánchez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Montánchez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Montánchez?
A: Montánchez offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 25 routes available. These trails cater to different fitness levels, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging climbs through the Sierra de Montánchez.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Montánchez?
A: The hiking trails in Montánchez traverse diverse landscapes. You'll find granite hills, low-lying pastures dotted with olive and fig trees, and higher sierra slopes covered with deciduous oak and chestnut woods. Notable natural features include the Montánchez Chestnut Grove and the Gorge of the Mills, offering varied scenery throughout the year.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Montánchez?
A: Yes, Montánchez has several easy hiking options. Out of the 25 available routes, 9 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These trails often explore the gentle pastures and lower slopes of the region.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest to see on a hike in Montánchez?
A: Hikers can explore several interesting landmarks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/745456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, often serving as a focal point or offering impressive views. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/722575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27)&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through historic mills, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Cross on the Camino&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique rock formations like the 'Mirador 'el cancho que se menea'.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Montánchez?
A: Yes, many routes in Montánchez are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990764535/castillo-de-montanchez-circular-by-the-ruta-de-las-aguas-pr-cc-30" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montánchez – circular by the Ruta de las Aguas (PR-CC 30)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.8 km path that incorporates water features and views of the castle.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Montánchez?
A: While hiking is possible year-round, the deciduous oak and chestnut woods offer a spectacular display, especially in autumn. Spring also brings pleasant temperatures and blooming flora. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Montánchez?
A: The hiking experience in Montánchez is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements like the castle and old mills, and the well-established network of paths.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Montánchez?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous adventure, there are challenging trails available. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10347561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez Castle – Montánchez Castle loop from Montánchez&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 12.6 km trail that circumnavigates the historic castle and involves significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find trails that feature water elements like gorges or rivers?
A: Yes, some routes in Montánchez incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990764535/castillo-de-montanchez-circular-by-the-ruta-de-las-aguas-pr-cc-30" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Aguas (PR-CC 30)&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a trail that includes water elements. The region also features the notable Gorge of the Mills, which is explored on some circular routes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the Montánchez Chestnut Grove?
A: Yes, the Montánchez Chestnut Grove is a significant natural feature that can be explored on several trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990771527/route-of-the-mills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads directly through this beautiful grove, offering scenic views and a pleasant hiking environment.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Montánchez?
A: Moderate hikes in Montánchez vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990771527/route-of-the-mills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.2 km, typically takes around 3 hours and 56 minutes to complete. Another moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990769582/montanchez-antennas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez Antennas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, is 8.6 km and takes approximately 2 hours and 35 minutes.
tips: A magnificent place to see and hear how the water falls in this old mill that in its time offered us numerous advances and that is currently obsolete. It's in ruins; but it allows you to appreciate its wall, the waterfall and the building where said mill was located.
One of the most beautiful routes that we can do in autumn are the routes through the chestnut groves.

faqs.question: Where does the Route of the Mills - circular through the Montánchez Chestnut Grove (PR-CC 27) start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts and ends near the Montánchez bullring. You'll find information panels for several local routes there. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the bullring in Montánchez.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this route?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike typically takes about 3 hours and 56 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Route of the Mills?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain, including ancient bridle paths, stone alleyways, and agricultural plots. A significant portion leads through the beautiful Montánchez Chestnut Grove. You'll also encounter paths along the Garganta de los Molinos and through picturesque olive groves.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several points of interest, including the historical mills scattered along the Garganta de los Molinos. A major highlight is the Castañar de Montánchez (Montánchez Chestnut Grove), providing a serene forest experience. You'll also enjoy views of the southern slopes of the Sierra de Montánchez and the town of Arroyomolinos in the background. The Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) – Sierra de Montánchez y Tamuja itself is a key feature.

faqs.question: Is the PR-CC 27 trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness due to some gradient. While not overly strenuous, it might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. It's best suited for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Mills - circular through the Montánchez Chestnut Grove (PR-CC 27) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, to respect wildlife, other hikers, and agricultural areas you might pass through.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Montánchez Chestnut Grove is particularly beautiful in autumn when the leaves change color. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush greenery. Summers can be hot, so if hiking then, start early and carry plenty of water.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra de Montánchez y Tamuja?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, nor are there any entrance fees to hike this trail or access the general area of the Sierra de Montánchez y Tamuja.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with other local trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Ruta de la Vaqueriza and Ruta de donde nace, among others.

faqs.question: What essential items should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and lack of water sources, it's essential to carry sufficient water. Sturdy footwear, such as hiking boots or slippers, and walking sticks are highly recommended for stability on varied terrain. A GPS device is also advisable due to numerous junctions.

faqs.question: Are there any water sources or facilities along the trail?
faqs.answer: There are no fountains or official water sources directly along the route, so it is crucial to carry all the water you will need for the entire hike. Plan accordingly, especially on warmer days.

waypoints.name: Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) – Sierra de Montánchez y Tamuja
